lines: Patient is a 65-year-old male with a history of hypertension and diabetes.
He presents with a 2-day history of fever, chills, and cough.
Vital signs: temperature 38.5 C, MAP 60 mmHg.
Labs: lactate 2.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL.
Patient is suspected to have pneumonia and possible sepsis.
Will start antibiotics and monitor vitals and labs closely.
Patient's platelet count is 80 10^9/L.
Patient is on vasopressors to maintain blood pressure.
Nursing: patient tolerating oral intake as ordered.
Nursing: skin warm and dry; no new rashes documented.
Nursing: pain score reviewed; analgesia as needed.
Provider: reviewed overnight events with bedside nurse.
Provider: active problems list updated in the chart.
Provider: medication reconciliation performed.
Provider: code status confirmed with patient or surrogate.
Patient's condition has not improved despite antibiotic treatment.
Labs: lactate 3.0 mmol/L, creatinine 1.5 mg/dL.
Vital signs: temperature 39.0 C, MAP 55 mmHg.
Patient's mental status has deteriorated, and he is now confused.
Will continue to monitor vitals and labs closely and consider changing antibiotics.
Patient's platelet count is 70 10^9/L.
Patient is still on vasopressors to maintain blood pressure.
Nursing: patient tolerating oral intake as ordered.
Nursing: skin warm and dry; no new rashes documented.
Nursing: pain score reviewed; analgesia as needed.
Provider: reviewed overnight events with bedside nurse.
Provider: active problems list updated in the chart.
Provider: medication reconciliation performed.
Provider: code status confirmed with patient or surrogate.
Labs: pending studies reviewed when available.
Patient was diagnosed with sepsis due to pneumonia.
He was treated with antibiotics and vasopressors.
Labs: lactate 1.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.0 mg/dL.
Vital signs: temperature 37.0 C, MAP 80 mmHg.
Patient's condition has improved, and he is being discharged.
Patient's platelet count is 100 10^9/L.
Patient is no longer on vasopressors.
Nursing: patient tolerating oral intake as ordered.
Nursing: skin warm and dry; no new rashes documented.
Nursing: pain score reviewed; analgesia as needed.
Provider: reviewed overnight events with bedside nurse.
Provider: active problems list updated in the chart.
Provider: medication reconciliation performed.
Provider: code status confirmed with patient or surrogate.
